title: Code of Conduct Lesson #1 — Youth Soccer Referee Abuse (Level 1)
source: Based on U.S. Soccer Policy 531-9, effective March 2025

questions.question: What percentage of new referees quit within 2–3 years?
According to the data in the course, what is the #1 reason referees cite for quitting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Low pay

answers.label: B
answers.text: Long travel distances

answers.label: C
answers.text: Verbal abuse from adults

answers.label: D
answers.text: Difficult game schedules

questions.question: How many calls are ever changed by yelling or threatening a referee?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A few, if the argument is convincing enough

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only calls made in the final minutes

answers.label: C
answers.text: Calls can be changed if a coach formally protests

answers.label: D
answers.text: Zero — no calls are ever changed by yelling or threats

questions.question: Under U.S. Soccer Policy 531-9, what is the minimum suspension for Non-Physical (Verbal) Level 1 Abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: 1-game suspension

answers.label: B
answers.text: 2-game suspension

answers.label: C
answers.text: 3-game suspension

answers.label: D
answers.text: 5-game suspension

questions.question: What is the minimum penalty for Physical Level 1 Abuse (minor or slight touching)?

answers.label: A
answers.text: 2-game suspension and a warning

answers.label: B
answers.text: 1-game suspension plus a fine

answers.label: C
answers.text: 3 games plus a ban of 1–6 months

answers.label: D
answers.text: Immediate lifetime ban

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of Non-Physical Level 1 Abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Raising your hand to get the referee's attention

answers.label: B
answers.text: Asking your coach to communicate with the referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: Saying 'Do you even know the rules?'

answers.label: D
answers.text: Applauding a referee's correct call

questions.question: According to Policy 531-9, which of the following physical actions constitutes abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Shaking the referee's hand after the match

answers.label: B
answers.text: Tapping the referee's shoulder to get their attention

answers.label: C
answers.text: Waving at the referee from the sideline

answers.label: D
answers.text: Pointing at the scoreboard

questions.question: Saying 'Are you blind? That was clearly offside!' is an example of which type of abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Physical Level 1 Abuse

answers.label: B
answers.text: Non-Physical Level 1 Abuse — Mocking Abilities

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse

answers.label: D
answers.text: Acceptable emotional expression

questions.question: What does Policy 531-9 say about comments intended as jokes that undermine a referee's authority?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Jokes are exempt if there is no hostile intent

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only repeated jokes are penalized

answers.label: C
answers.text: The policy applies to intent AND impact — jokes are not exempt

answers.label: D
answers.text: Jokes are only penalized if the referee files a formal complaint

questions.question: Saying 'What's the other team paying you?' is classified as which type of abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Physical Level 1 Abuse

answers.label: B
answers.text: Non-Physical Level 1 Abuse — Accusations of Bias

answers.label: C
answers.text: Non-Physical Level 1 Abuse — Mocking Abilities

answers.label: D
answers.text: Not abuse — expressing an opinion is protected speech

questions.question: Does Policy 531-9 cover non-verbal gestures, such as an exaggerated eye-roll after a call?

answers.label: A
answers.text: No, only spoken words are covered

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only if the gesture is caught on video

answers.label: C
answers.text: Yes, 'insinuating behavior' including gestures is covered

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only coaches are held to that standard, not spectators

questions.question: Which of the following gestures would NOT constitute Level 1 Abuse under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A theatrical head-in-hands gesture to shame the referee

answers.label: B
answers.text: A thumbs-down signal directed at the referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: An exaggerated eye-roll after a call

answers.label: D
answers.text: Clapping enthusiastically to encourage your own team

questions.question: Under Policy 531-9, what happens to penalties on a second offense?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The penalty stays the same

answers.label: B
answers.text: The person receives a formal written warning

answers.label: C
answers.text: The punishment doubles

answers.label: D
answers.text: The case is referred to national review

questions.question: What is the consequence for a third offense under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A 10-game suspension

answers.label: B
answers.text: A 1-year ban from all matches

answers.label: C
answers.text: Mandatory referee training course

answers.label: D
answers.text: Lifetime ban from all U.S. Soccer-affiliated matches

questions.question: Does the lifetime ban for a third offense apply only to players?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Yes, only registered players can be banned

answers.label: B
answers.text: No, it applies to coaches, parents, and spectators as well

answers.label: C
answers.text: It applies only to coaches and players, not spectators

answers.label: D
answers.text: It applies only to team officials, not individual parents

questions.question: What automatic multiplier applies when the offense is committed against a referee who is under 18?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Double the penalty

answers.label: B
answers.text: Triple the penalty

answers.label: C
answers.text: The standard penalty applies — age is not a factor

answers.label: D
answers.text: The penalty is increased by one game

questions.question: Which is the correct way to get a referee's attention without violating Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Tap the referee on the shoulder firmly but briefly

answers.label: B
answers.text: Grab the referee's jersey and wait for them to turn around

answers.label: C
answers.text: Raise your hand and wait for a stoppage in play

answers.label: D
answers.text: Step onto the field to speak with the referee directly

questions.question: Who is the appropriate person to seek a rules clarification from a referee during or after a match?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Any parent who witnessed the disputed call

answers.label: B
answers.text: The team captain on the field

answers.label: C
answers.text: The coach, through the correct channel after play stops

answers.label: D
answers.text: A spectator designated by the team

questions.question: Does Policy 531-9 provide a grace period or warning for first-time offenders?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Yes, everyone gets one free warning regardless of offense

answers.label: B
answers.text: Yes, but only for physical abuse offenses

answers.label: C
answers.text: No grace period — the full minimum penalty applies, except one warning per league for minor offenses

answers.label: D
answers.text: Yes, a one-game warning is always given before a suspension

questions.question: Saying 'You suck! You're the worst ref we've ever had' is classified as what under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Acceptable expression of frustration

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse, requiring a separate hearing

answers.label: C
answers.text: Non-Physical Level 1 Abuse — Dismissive and Degrading Language

answers.label: D
answers.text: Physical Level 1 Abuse because of the aggressive tone

questions.question: What effect does spectator abuse of referees have on children watching?

answers.label: A
answers.text: No effect — children understand adults express frustration differently

answers.label: B
answers.text: It teaches children that disrespect toward officials is acceptable

answers.label: C
answers.text: It motivates children to become better players

answers.label: D
answers.text: It has only an effect if the child is on the field

questions.question: Which of the following IS considered an acceptable way to express disagreement with a referee call?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Shouting 'That call is ridiculous!' from the sideline

answers.label: B
answers.text: Giving a loud, sarcastic clap directed at the referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: Saying 'Tough call, ref' and moving on

answers.label: D
answers.text: Repeatedly questioning the same call from the sideline

questions.question: What does the course recommend if you witness referee abuse from another spectator?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Ignore it — it's not your responsibility

answers.label: B
answers.text: Confront the abusive spectator loudly

answers.label: C
answers.text: Record it on your phone and post it online

answers.label: D
answers.text: Report the abuse — the policy only works if abuse is reported

questions.question: What does the course identify as the key reason perceived referee bias 'rarely holds up on review'?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Referees are always neutral and never make mistakes

answers.label: B
answers.text: Referees cannot see everything from one angle

answers.label: C
answers.text: Video review always proves parents wrong

answers.label: D
answers.text: Bias claims are inadmissible without video evidence

questions.question: Which of the following best summarizes the core message of Code of Conduct Lesson #1?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Referees should expect criticism as part of their job

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only physical contact with a referee constitutes punishable abuse

answers.label: C
answers.text: Verbal and physical abuse of referees carries real penalties, drives officials away, and harms youth soccer for everyone

answers.label: D
answers.text: Parents should stay silent during matches to avoid any risk of suspension
